title: History Editing, Rebase, and Selective Transfer · Conflict Triage
summary: Prepare a reviewable local series while preserving authorship intent, conflict evidence, and a recovery reference. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a history-rewrite plan and verification graph and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Explain rebase as selecting commits, resetting a base, and replaying new commits with new identities.
Use pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, drop, exec, and reorder with dependency and test awareness.
Limit history rewriting using collaboration state, force-with-lease, recovery refs, and explicit coordination.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary. Competency probe: Explain rebase as selecting commits, resetting a base, and replaying new commits with new identities.

Inspect the Rebase Replay trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain rebase as selecting commits, resetting a base, and replaying new commits with new identities.

content.text: For Rebase Replay, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Rebase Replay precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary. Competency probe: Explain rebase as selecting commits, resetting a base, and replaying new commits with new identities.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Rebase moves existing commit objects without changing their IDs.

options.text: For inspect the failure record, apply Rebase Replay without its stated preconditions, omit the a history-rewrite plan and verification graph trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Rebase Replay for a history-rewrite plan and verification graph.
The record names the assumption, applies Rebase Replay, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Rebase Replay record says a history-rewrite plan and verification graph looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve inspect the failure record.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-rewrite-verification-94mxnz" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rewrite Verification into observable performance evidence.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Rewrite Verification structural check is only one gate. a history-rewrite plan and verification graph must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: Name the Rewrite Verification precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary. Competency probe: Compare old and rewritten ranges by patch, tree, tests, metadata, and range-diff before replacing a shared reference.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Matching final files proves a rewritten series preserves review meaning.

content.title: Evidence defense
